title: PHP Data Types
content:
### PHP Data Types

PHP supports several data types that determine what kind of data a variable can hold.

### Scalar Types

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `string` | Sequence of characters | `"Hello"` |
| `int` | Whole numbers | `42` |
| `float` | Decimal numbers | `3.14` |
| `bool` | True or false | `true` |

### Compound Types

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `array` | Ordered map | `[1, 2, 3]` |
| `object` | Instance of a class | `new stdClass()` |
| `callable` | Callable function | `function() {}` |
| `iterable` | Iterable value | array or Traversable |

### Special Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `null` | Variable with no value |
| `resource` | External resource (file handle) |

```php
<?php
$str = "Hello";
$int = 100;
$float = 10.5;
$bool = true;
$arr = [1, 2, 3];
$null = null;
echo gettype($int); // "integer"
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Use `var_dump()` to see both the type and value of any variable.

quiz.question: Which function returns the data type of a variable?
